lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS224N: NLP w/ DL | Spring 2024 | Lecture 7 - Attention, Final Projects and LLM Intro

unit.name: Attention Variants and the Road to Transformers
unit.description: Compares dot-product, bilinear/multiplicative, low-rank, and additive attention, then connects these choices to transformer-style projected dot-product attention and positional-information questions.
unit.summary: The segment surveys how attention scores can be computed beyond plain dot products. Dot-product attention is simple but crude because an LSTM hidden state stores many kinds of information, not all of which should matter equally for lookup [ts=1556s] [ts=1626s]. Bilinear or multiplicative attention inserts a learned matrix between encoder and decoder states so the model can learn which subspaces should align, even when corresponding information lives in different dimensions [ts=1639s] [ts=1708s]. A low-rank factorization of that matrix leads to projecting vectors into a smaller space before applying dot-product attention, which the lecturer explicitly previews as the transformer pattern [ts=1784s] [ts=1866s]. He also reviews additive attention as the original Bahdanau formulation using a small neural net for score computation, notes the tradeoff between quality and speed, and explains why RNN-based attention did not require extra positional encodings in the same way transformers do [ts=1890s] [ts=2168s].

key_points.text: Plain dot-product attention compares the full encoder and decoder hidden states directly.
Bilinear attention adds a learned matrix so relevant features can align across different dimensions.
Low-rank bilinear attention is equivalent to projecting vectors into a lower-dimensional space and then taking a dot product.
Additive attention computes scores with a small neural network over encoder and decoder states.
In practice, projected dot-product style attention won because it is much simpler and faster.
RNN encoder states already carry order information, so extra positional information was not essential in this setup.
